meta.description: Create character identity with prompt tokens and voice config

step.name: receive_character_description
step.action: Receive the character description from the user or script. Capture: name, role, personality traits, physical appearance, age range, distinguishing features, and any specific visual or voice requirements. If referencing an existing character for modification, load that character's current identity.json.

step.name: load_schema_and_defaults
step.action: Load the character identity schema from {project-root}/_cookie/schemas/character-identity.schema.json. Load default values from {project-root}/_cookie/_config/character-defaults.yaml. These provide the required field structure and sensible fallback values for optional fields.
step.output: Schema definition and default values loaded

step.name: create_identity_json
step.action: Create the identity.json file following the schema. Populate: id (kebab-case slug), name, role, description, personality array, physical_description object (height, build, skin_tone, hair, eyes, distinguishing_features), age_range, and metadata (created_at, version, status).

step.name: define_prompt_tokens
step.action: Define the prompt_tokens object with: core_identity (a concise, front-loadable description for AI generation prompts, max 50 tokens), style_modifiers (array of style-specific tokens), negative_tokens (what this character should never look like), and consistency_anchors (key visual elements that must remain consistent across all generations). Core identity tokens must be descriptive enough to generate recognizable, consistent results.

step.name: define_voice_config
step.action: Define the voice configuration: voice_type (narration, dialogue, both, none), provider (elevenlabs, system), voice_id (ElevenLabs voice ID if applicable), voice_settings (stability, similarity_boost, style, speed), speaking_style (conversational, dramatic, instructional), and voice_description (natural language description for reference). Skip if character has no speaking role.

step.name: set_active_style_and_outfit
step.action: Define at least one style entry with: style_id, style_name, description, and outfit entries. Each outfit includes: outfit_id, outfit_name, description, and prompt_tokens specific to the outfit. Set one style and outfit as active_style and active_outfit respectively.

step.name: create_directory_structure
step.action: Create the character directory structure: characters/{char-id}/identity.json, characters/{char-id}/voice-config.json, characters/{char-id}/styles/{style-id}/ (for reference images to be generated later). Ensure the directory naming follows kebab-case convention.

step.name: save_identity_and_voice
step.action: Save the complete identity.json to characters/{char-id}/identity.json. Save the voice configuration to characters/{char-id}/voice-config.json. Both files must validate against their respective schemas.

step.name: validate_with_t7
step.action: Run the T7 character validation task against the newly created character. Verify: schema compliance, required field completeness, prompt token quality (descriptive enough for consistent generation), voice config completeness, and directory structure correctness. Fix any validation errors before proceeding.

step.name: mark_ready_for_references
step.action: Update the character's status to "pending-references" indicating that the identity is defined but reference images have not yet been generated. The generate-character-refs workflow will pick up characters in this status.
step.output: Character status updated, ready for reference image generation

quality-gates.gate: identity.json validates against the character identity schema
All required fields are present and populated
prompt_tokens.core_identity is descriptive enough for consistent AI generation
Voice config is complete if the character has a speaking role
Directory structure follows the expected convention
